summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on April 5, 2022, addressing three zoning cases. They approved a final development plan for TPT Holding at 6600 Patricia Boulevard, approved a zone change to PUD for Green Girl Garden landscaping business at 1591 Petz Avenue with conditions including a driveway agreement with neighboring property owners, and approved a PDO plan for Stole Properties LLC at 1636 State Route 28 with multiple conditions including paving, screening, and landscaping requirements.
decisions: ZC2021-007: Approved final development plan for TPT Holding at 6600 Patricia Boulevard with Board of Trustees conditions
ZC2022-001: Approved zone change to PUD for Green Girl Garden at 1591 Petz Avenue with conditions including mutual driveway agreement with neighboring property owners
ZC2022-002: Approved PDO plan for Stole Properties LLC at 1636 State Route 28 with staff conditions including paving, privacy fencing, screening, landscaping, and other requirements
action_items: Mears Tucker (Green Girl Garden) to work out a mutual driveway maintenance agreement with neighboring property owners on the private drive
Mears Tucker to provide documentation regarding legal ingress/egress to the subject property at 1591 Petz Avenue
Stole Properties LLC to comply with all staff conditions including paving, privacy fencing, screening, landscaping, exterior lighting shielding, and cross access easement
Board of Trustees to revoke the erroneously issued zoning permit for 1636 State Route 28
Gary to send email to commission members if there are cases for next meeting
ZC2022-001 and ZC2022-002 to go before Board of Trustees on April 26, 2022
key_amounts: 6,720 square feet per building at 1636 State Route 28 (13,440 total for both buildings)
10 proposed parking spaces for Stole Properties at 1636 State Route 28
ISR change from 9.4% to 16.7% at 1636 State Route 28
15-foot ingress/egress utility easement at 1591 Petz Avenue
6-foot minimum to 8-foot maximum privacy fencing required (multiple cases)
Clermont County Planning Commission recommended approval 7-0 for ZC2022-002
10% gross area to be preserved as green space for ZC2022-002
notable_topics: Final development plan review for TPT Holding at 6600 Patricia Boulevard (PBDD)
Zone change from B2 to PUD for Green Girl Garden landscaping business at 1591 Petz Avenue
Private driveway access and maintenance concerns from neighboring property owners on Petz Lane
PDO State Route 28 overlay district zone change for Stole Properties LLC at 1636 State Route 28
Revocation of erroneously issued zoning permit for 1636 State Route 28
Building face material relief from 80% brick/stone/split face block/EFIS requirement
attendees: Mears Tucker (applicant, Green Girl Garden, 1591 State Route 28)
Sheila Pageant (property owner, 6130 Doe Court, Loveland)
Teresa Pettigrew Stevens-Burring (property owner, 1531 Fay Road)
Christie Wells (property owner, 6985 Edenton Pleasant Plain Road)
Attorney for Dan Ketterling-Rowart (6429 Christian Boulevard)
notes: Meeting started at 6:32 PM and adjourned at 7:41 PM. Only three of five commission members were present (Dinwiddie and Frisbee absent). The ZC2022-001 case generated significant public concern from neighboring property owners about private driveway maintenance and access. The private drive at Petz Lane was noted as historically called Petz Lane, not Petz Avenue. Some neighboring property owners claimed they did not receive notification letters, only deceased family members did. The applicant for ZC2022-002 (Stole Properties LLC) was not present at the meeting. Audio quality issues apparent throughout the transcript with repeated 'Board of Trustees Meeting' placeholder text suggesting recording/transcription problems.
title: Gosen Zoning Mtg 4-5-22
